zotero.abstractNote: Training Data Attribution (TDA) methods aim to identify which training examples are most influential for a model's prediction. While TDA has seen significant technical progress, its utility for end-users remains under-explored. In this paper, we argue that the current research trajectory in TDA is overly focused on technical benchmarks, often neglecting the needs and cognitive limitations of human users. We propose a shift towards user-focused TDA research, which prioritizes human interpretability, decision-making support, and trust. We outline a research agenda for human-centered TDA, highlighting key challenges and opportunities for future work.
